title: phyllanthus attenuatus subsp. attenuatus
intro: Phyllanthus attenuatus subsp. attenuatus, also known as the New Zealand cheeseling or New Zealand matipo, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a shrub native to New Zealand, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, often used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and liver disease.
